categories.name: Iconic Symbols & Culture
categories.description: Distinctive Dutch cultural icons and symbols, including windmills, tulips, cycling culture, and contributions to art and history.

categories.name: Food & Drink
categories.description: Traditional and popular Dutch cuisine, featuring both savory dishes and sweet treats, along with local beverages.

categories.name: Everyday Life & Habits
categories.description: Common Dutch customs, lifestyle patterns, and cultural behaviors that define daily life in the Netherlands.

categories.name: Inventions
categories.description: Notable scientific and technological contributions made by the Dutch to the world.

things.name: Cycling
things.description: A primary mode of transport, with bike paths everywhere.

things.name: Dutch Masters Art
things.description: Famous painters like Rembrandt and Vermeer.

things.name: Orange (Oranje)
things.description: The national color, linked to the Royal Family.

things.name: Gezelligheid
things.description: A cozy, convivial atmosphere (hard to translate directly).

things.name: Gouda & Edam Cheese
things.description: Famous dairy products, often eaten with bread.

things.name: Hagelslag
things.description: Chocolate sprinkles for breakfast on bread.

things.name: Oliebollen
things.description: Dutch doughnuts, especially around New Year.

things.name: AVG Dinner
things.description: A simple meal of Aardappelen (potatoes), Vlees (meat), Groenten (vegetables).

things.name: Doe normaal
things.description: Act normal - a cultural emphasis on modesty and common sense.

things.name: Indonesian Influence
things.description: Dishes like rijsttafel (rice table) are popular due to history.

things.name: Orange Carrots
things.description: The Dutch cultivated them to be orange.
